message:
feat(fuzz): add fuzz_socket for POSIX socket surface (bd-dvr22 priority-3)

Adds crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_socket.rs — stateful
harness covering socket/socketpair/bind/listen/accept/accept4/connect/
send/recv/sendto/recvfrom/shutdown/setsockopt/getsockopt/getsockname/
getpeername with 16 op variants.

Handle table (MAX_SOCKETS=6, MAX_OPS=12 per iteration) tracks Live vs
Stale sockets so later ops in a sequence can operate on already-
closed fds; the stale-fd invariant (op on Stale slot must return -1)
is asserted on every touched op.

Safety constraints that make this harness CI-safe:
- AF_UNIX only, SOCK_STREAM or SOCK_DGRAM — never AF_INET/AF_INET6,
  so no real network traffic leaves the process.
- Bind / connect addresses are abstract-namespace (sun_path[0]=0,
  per-iteration unique `flc-fz-<hex>` tail from a static AtomicU64),
  so no filesystem paths are created or left behind.
- Send / recv buffers bounded to 1 KiB; ops use MSG_DONTWAIT +
  MSG_NOSIGNAL so the fuzzer never blocks or raises SIGPIPE into the
  fuzz binary.
- accept4 is driven with SOCK_NONBLOCK only (plain accept on
  listening sockets would block libFuzzer's iteration clock).
- Global SOCKLOCK serializes iterations.

Oracle is crash-detector + rc-in-contract + stale-fd lifecycle
invariant. A dlsym-based differential against host libc is tracked
as follow-up per the same dlsym(RTLD_NEXT) caveat as fuzz_env /
fuzz_pathname / fuzz_sched / fuzz_pidfd_timer: both our ABI and
libc export the same `socket`/`bind`/... symbol names, so naive
extern "C" binds to only one impl.

Validation: cargo build --bin fuzz_socket clean (2.6s); cargo clippy
--bin fuzz_socket --no-deps -- -D warnings clean.

Continues bd-dvr22 drain (mmap → signal → socket). Remaining
priorities: fortify, setjmp, dlfcn, pthread (TSan), c11threads
(TSan).

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -70,6 +70,11 @@ name = "fuzz_signal"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_signal.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_socket"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_socket.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_resolver"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resolver.rs"
